results.title: Convert your drum recordings into PDF, MIDI, and MusicXML sheet music
results.content:
### Universe

€14/month

~~€348~~ €168 billed annually

Transcribe and learn on all apps

## Frequently Asked Questions

### What is DrumConvert?

DrumConvert converts audio into drum sheet music, allowing you to capture a groove, analyze a pattern, or prepare a cover version. It’s particularly useful for learning a rhythm part, creating a MIDI drum track, or working on a song with a metronome. Exports in PDF, MIDI, and MusicXML formats allow you to read the sheet music or import it directly into a DAW.

### How does DrumConvert work?

Paste a YouTube link or import an audio/video file, then start the transcription. DrumConvert analyzes the rhythmic content and generates a structured drum score. Then download the PDF for playback, or MIDI/MusicXML for editing and production. [...] ### What does the generated score contain?

You get a rhythmic score with tempo and measures, designed to be played or covered. MIDI is particularly useful if you want to edit the pattern, quantize it, or integrate it into a production.

### Is the transcription accurate?

DrumConvert uses AI to maximize accuracy, but the result depends on the mix and style (double kick, ghost notes, breakbeats, etc.). A preview allows you to validate before exporting, then you can refine in MusicXML/MIDI-compatible software if you are looking for a highly detailed final rendering.

### How much does it cost?

The first 30 seconds are free. To convert in full: Solo $9/month, Galaxy $19/month, Universe $29/month (with conversion quotas).

## Get the sheet music for your favorite drum tunes now [...] ## Paste the YouTube link

## Drums are not your Thing? No Problem!

## Known from



How does Drum2Notes work?

Drum2Notes can turn the drums of any song into notes, within seconds! Simply upload an audio file, paste in a YouTube link or record your instrument directly. Our AI then listens to the drums and transcribes them automatically into notes, which are displayed as sheet music. You can then edit your transcription and download it in different formats.

Is Drum2Notes for free?

You can do as many Demo Transcriptions with Drum2Notes as you like, which are completely for free! This allows you to test the quality before upgrading to full transcriptions, which also unlock many more features!

Find out more in this article.

How precise does Drum2Notes transcribe music?

The better the quality of the music, the better the transcription – it’s as simple as that! In other words, if a professional transcriber will have a hard time to transcribe a certain part, then so will Drum2Notes. But even if there are some mistakes in your transcribed notes, you’ve saved tons of time with Drum2Notes and the Edit Mode lets you fix these mistakes with a couple clicks.

Find out more in this article.

results.title: Is there any software or AI tool that can accurately transcribe drum parts from an audio file into proper drum notation
results.content:
- Short answer is no. Longer answer is "maybe poorly, but you're going to spend more time or money correcting it than it would take to just do it from scratch". That's the problem with this idea, inherently. Even if you get an ML solution that has 99.9% accuracy (which is very hard to do), one mistake in 1000 is still not acceptable for many applications (that's in 1000 "notation components" btw, like notes, rests, slurs, articulation, dynamics, hand splitting (on piano), etc, not 1000 notes), and it takes as long or longer to proofread at a level of detail to catch 1 mistake in 1000 than it does to just transcribe it from scratch.
- Theres no tech that can do this properly and accurately. You're in the right subreddit to find a real person to do it for you, though! [...] - Drumagogue can pull it to midi you might need to clean up to convert to notation.
- Yea, me. You’re gonna need a human to do this. There’s too much nuance and subtly for a computer/AI tool to do this with any degree of accuracy. [...] edge on the signal. Thats probably the least amount of work that will give you a decent midi file to start with. Then you just check every note and move them to different samples using one of those beatmap synths with one sound for each note.

results.title: Drum Score Editor - Drumming On Demand
results.content:
Beautify your score – with a single keystroke (or menu click) you can have the notes in your score be moved to where they should be, given their value and the time signature of the piece
 There’s loads more features and options enabled, head over to the documentation to go through the detail or contact me – always happy to chat about Drum Score Editor! [...] Font-tastic beautiful music and text – a music font as used by the big professional music notation software is enabled, as well as any text font on your system with stunning results
 Produce mp4 movies of your score with a visual indication of tempo as the beat notes are indicated, with optional click track, and the ability for you to record your audio track so your corps can see and hear the music exactly how you want it expressed
 Rudiment Libraries – often used phrases and rudiments can be dragged and dropped onto any number of libraries, which can be organised as you like, e.g. a 2/4 library, a strathspey phrase library
 Bar content guidance, warns you if there too few or too many notes in a bar [...] ## About Drum Score Editor

Drum Score Editor is free software for drum scores in the pipe band world and is available on Apple macOS, Microsoft Windows and Linux. There is no charge to download and use the community edition of this software, it’s distributed under a Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial-NoDerivs 3.0 Unported License.

There is a paid license available which unlocks advanced features that will save even more time writing and maintaining beautiful scores for a drum corps. More details about this are available in the Licensing section below, however the free version is very capable and many people regard this still as the most rapid way to work with drum scores. They haven’t seen how rapid they would be with the advanced features!
